content:
Hello,

I notice it is forbidden to call directly "bindings::foo" in the driver code. Meanwhile, there are still lots of header files not wrapped in the rust/kernel/, which makes some key functions in the kernel unavailable, just like the "cpumask_t" struct in the include/linux/cpumask.h.

If I want to use these unavailable functions or structs In the Linux kernel, should I create a file in the rust/kernel/foo.rs and wrap the corresponding items? I wonder whether this is a correct way to solve the problem. Furthermore, should I submit the foo.rs to the kernel community as a patch?
I just search the message history. I think the answer is a yes. So I will close this question.
